meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Setup to Beat Project Block
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. Learn how to use Claude Code and Antigravity to build production-ready apps as a student. Start your journey here.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from a lack of technical leverage. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform abstract concepts into functional codebases instantly. This workflow enables you to prototype complex SaaS features in minutes, shifting your focus from syntax struggles to architectural mastery and rapid, iterative development.
content.intro:
You stare at a blinking cursor, the weight of a blank IDE pressing against your ambition. You know how to code, but you lack the 'what'—the spark that turns a student assignment into a portfolio-grade asset. This is the valley of death for most developers: the transition from tutorial-hell to independent engineering.

But what if you could outsource the architectural heavy lifting? By leveraging Claude Code as an autonomous pair programmer and Antigravity for rapid scaffolding, you bypass the friction of boilerplate. This isn't just about finishing a project; it's about building a high-velocity development system that turns raw curiosity into deployed software.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Stop building 'to-do' lists. Use Antigravity to brainstorm niche SaaS problems that solve real-world pain points for specific user personas.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Identify 3 untapped SaaS niches for students that solve specific workflow problems. Provide a feature list for an MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --prompt 'Initialize a project structure based on the MVP feature list generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architecting the Tech Stack
steps.description: Translate your idea into a robust folder structure. We focus on modern, scalable stacks like Next.js and Supabase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a directory structure and tech stack recommendation for a scalable SaaS MVP. Include folder organization for components, hooks, and API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create the folder structure and install necessary dependencies for a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Scaffolding Core Features
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate for authentication and database schemas to eliminate manual setup time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Supabase schema for a user-authenticated dashboard. Include tables for profiles, projects, and subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --write 'Implement Supabase auth middleware and user profile database connection using the provided schema.'

steps.title: Iterative UI Development
steps.description: Rapidly build out the interface. Focus on high-impact components that provide immediate user value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive, minimalist dashboard UI component using Shadcn/UI for the project management overview.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate the UI components for the dashboard header and the project listing grid.'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Push to production and use automated feedback loops to refine the application based on real interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted SaaS application. Include performance monitoring and error logging setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Configure Vercel build settings and integrate Sentry for error tracking.'

pro_tips: Always use Claude Code in an isolated branch to prevent polluting your main codebase during rapid experimentation.
Treat Antigravity prompts as 'Product Requirements Documents' (PRDs) to keep your code focused on the business problem.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your components before you even finish the implementation logic.
Refactor your codebase every 3 sessions to prevent 'Technical Debt Creep' common in AI-assisted development.
Keep a log of Claude Code sessions to track your architectural decision-making process for future interviews.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, share the link in our Discord channel for peer review, and start iterating based on user feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the perfect process. Your portfolio is only as strong as the problems you solve. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
